example: Keep your chin up, I’m sure you’ll find a better job soon.
hint: не унывать, когда всё идет не по плану

example: I had to burn the midnight oil to finish the presentation.

meaning: иметь одинаковое понимание ситуации или соглашаться
example: Let’s have a meeting to make sure we’re all on the same page.
The budget deficit is the elephant in the room that no one mentions.
hint: большая проблема, о которой боятся говорить

meaning: идти по ложному следу, ошибаться в причинах
example: If you think I’m responsible for this, you’re barking up the wrong tree.
hint: искать решение не там, где нужно

example: The exam was a piece of cake; I finished it in ten minutes.
He said he was fine, but reading between the lines, he’s upset.
We’ve been working for ten hours—let’s call it a day.
hint: прекратить что-то делать, потому что устал

example: He told a joke to break the ice at the start of the meeting.

idiom: hit the nail on the head
translation: попасть не в бровь, а в глаз
meaning: сказать или сделать что-то совершенно точно
example: You hit the nail on the head when you said we need a new strategy.
We missed the boat on that investment—the price is too high now.
My boss and I don’t always see eye to eye on marketing.

idiom: costs an arm and a leg
example: Repairing the engine cost an arm and a leg.

meaning: приболеть или быть не в духе
example: I'm feeling a bit under the weather, so I'll stay home today.
